title: Forms from Cerebral 1.0.1
author: Reuben1987AI

title: [BUG] @Cerebral/react broken for 4.3.0
author: zachrog
body:
With the latest update for @Cerebral/react with 4.3.0 the entire package no longer works as the import pathing is incorrect in the output files. The two incorrect imports are located in HOC.jsx and conatiner.jsx. 

In the repo the import that is broken is: 

```import { ControllerContext } from '@cerebral/react/src/context' ```

When the project is built, the import is not translated into relative imports so keeps @cerebral/react/src/context when the actual location is @cerebral/react/lib/context or ./context.

This breaks the entire package and does not allow cerebral to be used.

Error from local repo using @cerebral/react.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c0f9b186-318b-48a3-afd2-5cdbdba52e5b)



The way to fix this is to make the import ```import { ControllerContext } from './context' ```

title: `addModule` doesn't initialize reactions
author: fmal
body:
I noticed that when adding a module definition that has some reactions defined, the reaction handlers are not firing. Digging deeper i have found out that reactions are created with `.create()` method https://github.com/cerebral/cerebral/blob/next/packages/node_modules/cerebral/src/Module.js#L120 but they're not initialized. Initialization (with `.initialize()` method) happens only on https://github.com/cerebral/cerebral/blob/next/packages/node_modules/cerebral/src/Controller.js#L51 root module/controller creation only for statically defined modules. Dynamically added modules with `addModule` don't get initialized (similarly `removeModule` should destroy them i believe).
This is contrary to reactions in views https://github.com/cerebral/cerebral/blob/next/packages/node_modules/cerebral/src/View.js#L251 which are initialized upon creation and destroyed when unmounting: https://github.com/cerebral/cerebral/blob/next/packages/node_modules/cerebral/src/View.js#L145.

title: parallel does not work as expected
author: bernhardreisenberger
body:
Hi,

When using equals in a sequence, and executing a subsequence in parallel, the actions in this subsequence are only executed in parallel when i wrap the subsequence in square brackets.

> [
>       equals(state.foo),
>       {
>         // not wrapped in [] -> wrong behavior
>         bar: parallel([sayHey, sayHo])
>       }
>     ]

> [
>       equals(state.foo),
>       {
>         // wrapped in [] -> correct behavior
>         bar: [parallel([sayHey, sayHo])]
>       }
>     ]

see https://codesandbox.io/s/parallel-bug-pi1mc for an example.

Not using equals, both sequences (wrapped and unwrapped) will behave the same.
It is a minor issue, but one to easily trip over.

title: access dotted keys
author: bernhardreisenberger
body:
This is a reference to #1321 .

I guess this was asked before, but i cannot find the answer.

`set(state.domains['example.com'], 'example.com') ` will result in the following error:
> "factory.set(state\`domains.example.com\`, example.com)" has an error: Cannot read property 'com' of undefined

Is there a way, to have keys with dot in it?

title: Cerebral 5.2.0: inferno does render child before parent
author: bernhardreisenberger
body:
hi.

With the latest release 5.2.0 of Cerebral there is an issue with Inferno.

It may happen, that the child component will be rendered before the parent component. This is bad, if the parent defines the visibility of the child components.

In my application, i have one modal (parent component). only this modal component is used on the whole website. to display different content, i use child components, and switch them as needed. for simplicity, i check the visibility of the modal in the parent component.
I tried my best by providing a simplified example, visualizing the problem.
https://codesandbox.io/s/parent-child-bug-jhcdz

In version 5.1.1 it worked fine this way, but with 5.2.0 it will randomly break.
The problem does not occur with react, so i guess it is caused somewhere in the view package

title: Cerebral v4: Deprecated React lifecycle methods
author: JohannesAnd
body:
The following lifecycle methods are currently causing deprecation warnings with React v16.9
- componentWillMount
- componentWillRecieveProps
- componentWillUpdate

as they are added in the HOC used by connect.

Are there any plans to fix this and make cerebral compatible with upcoming React v17?

EDIT: I see this has already been fixed in cerebral v5. Any plans of fixing it for v4, or do we simply have to migrate?

title: Setting non-existent path in state works without devtools, throws error with devtools
author: aultac
body:
When setting a non-existent path in the state (using a tag and store.set), if devtools is turned off the path in the state is created appropriately.  When devtools is turned on, the browser throws an error about reading a property of undefined on line 93 of Store.js in cerebral.  The Cerebral debugger shows the value in the state as if it had been set appropriately in the first place.

To reproduce, simply do this in any action with devtools enabled in your app
```store.set(state`path.does.not.exist`, 'hello');```
This will throw an error in the console that "does" does not exist.  Now look in the debugger and you will see a top-level state key named `path` whose value is an object that contains a key named `does`, .... and so on ... just as you would have expected.  However, the browser code threw the error and stopped.  If you catch the error and follow up with a get(state`path.does.not.exist`), you get undefined even though the debugger shows it in the state.

Then disable devtools and repeat, and you will see that it works as expected.

As a workaround, you can manually loop through and create all the elements of the path one at a time in your app and it will work, but this is messy.

title: When using devtools, cannot change store state using string path.
author: cyrusbowman
body:
When using devtools I get the following error:

> You are extracting a path from an argument that is not a Tag.

Using a tag instead of a string ie. `store.set(state`title`, 'Bacon')` works as expected.

@cerebral/react v4.1.1
cerebral: v5.1.1


Action:
```
function changeTitle({store}) {
  store.set('title', 'Bacon')
}
```

![CerebralError](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2474343/65654553-3ddf6200-dfe7-11e9-9f02-2c64020b7a69.gif)

title: A certain combination of feature usage causes forceUpdate() on an unmounted component
author: atanas-dev
body:
Repro case:
https://codesandbox.io/s/koz63xy3ro

Open the console, click on the unmount button **once** and wait a second - the "update on unmounted component" error will show up.

Key points to reproduce this:
1.Have computed state that depends on **2 OR MORE** other computed states. This does not work when it only requires a single other computed state value. In the repro case `someComputed` uses `someInternalComputed1` and `someInternalComputed2` which yield the same value - `someState`.
2. Connect component A to `someComputed`.
3. Have component B which conditionally renders component A based on some state value which we will flip to unmount component A.
4. Have a sequence which consists of:
  1. Any async action. In the repro case I used `wait(1000)`.
  2. Flip the state which will cause component A to be unmounted
  3. Change the state that powers all of the internal computed state.
5. Run the sequence.

It is quite convoluted but I currently have this issue in a real world project.

title: Change in computed state does not trigger reactions
author: atanas-dev
body:
A reaction depending only on a computed state will never be called. I encountered this locally and reproduced it based on the counter sandbox example:
https://codesandbox.io/s/2k962vopn

As you can see, changes to the computed value do trigger a component render (you can even remove the `count` state dependency and it will still render) but do not trigger the reaction (neither `reactionCalls` increments nor `console.log` gets called). 

If you add `count: state'count',` to the reaction state dependencies the reaction will get called (this is the current workaround I have to use for my project).
